text: What happens when water gets too much fertilizer? Eutrophication occurs when nutrient runoff boosts algae growth in lakes and rivers. But here's the catch: too much algae blocks sunlight and oxygen for other life forms. This leads to dead zones where fish and plants can’t survive, impacting entire ecosystems. Understanding eutrophication helps us combat water pollution and protect our water bodies. Want to learn more about our planet? Follow for more scientific insights!
